title: Fail to generate swagger.yaml
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
Fail to generate swagger.yaml

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. get the go-swagger with version v0.26.1
2.  make swagger

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
swagger generate spec -o swagger.yaml -i tags.yaml -w ./ -m
2021/02/08 15:25:23 WARNING: Missing embedded parser for a *types.Basic, skipping model
2021/02/08 15:25:23 WARNING: Missing embedded parser for a *types.Basic, skipping model
2021/02/08 15:25:23 WARNING: Missing embedded parser for a *types.Basic, skipping model
2021/02/08 15:25:23 WARNING: Missing embedded parser for a *types.Basic, skipping model
unsupported type "invalid type"
make[1]: *** [Makefile:12: swagger.yaml] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/mnt/data/src/ray_application/podman/pkg/api'
make: *** [Makefile:252: pkg/api/swagger.yaml] Error 2


**Describe the results you expected:**
The file is successfully generated. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

I have tried using the master and version 2.2.1. They both fail. 

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
I'm using ubuntu 20.04 LTS physical machine.

comments.body: @photoszzt i just tried this on my laptop (fedora) by rebuilding the latest go-swagger from their github repo.  It worked perfectly.  Could you tell me more about your environment.  Did you use their static build? How did you get go-swagger onto your system?

@baude I try both "go get" and download the static build. They both fail the same way. The go compiler I'm using is 
go version go1.15.8 linux/amd64

It worked perfectly for me using 20.04's default golang which is 1.13. I used both a compiled version of go-swagger and their static binary. Perhaps it is your golang binaries that have a problem?  That said, I am using golang-1.15 on Fedora with no problem.  I'm not sure we can call this a Podman issue at this point?
I'm using the binary I directly download from go's website(https://golang.org/dl/).
@baude I tried both 1.15.8 and 1.14.15, and fail the same way.
@photoszzt I'll look again in the morning, but again, I don't think is a podman issue at all. if you can join #podman on freenode we might be able to live debug the issue collaboratively.
@baude Sure. I can join freenode tomorrow. I also try the ubuntu's go and also fail. I also try using a fresh ubuntu 20.04 container, install the dependencies and it also fails.

@photoszzt try this:

```
FROM docker.io/library/ubuntu:20.04

# Install various packages we need from Ubuntu
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y make git wget && apt-get clean all

# Install latest golang binaries from upstream
RUN wget -qO- https://golang.org/dl/go1.15.8.linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xvz -C /usr/local

# Set environment path to pick up new golang
ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:${PATH}"

# Get and build go-swagger and pull upstream podman
RUN go get -u github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger/cmd/swagger && git clone http://github.com/containers/podman /root/go/src/github.com/containers/podman

# Set the workdir for convenience
WORKDIR /root/go/src/github.com/containers/podman
```

1. Make a Containerfile from the above.
2. podman build -t swagger .
3. podman run -it --rm swagger /bin/bash
4. make swagger
@baude Thanks for the steps. It's working now.

@baude By the way, this is the container setup I try that fail:
```
From ubuntu:focal
ENV TZ=America/Chicago
RUN ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime && echo $TZ > /etc/timezone
RUN apt-get -y update && apt-get -y install \
    git \
    golang-go \
    go-md2man \
    iptables \
    libassuan-dev \
    libbtrfs-dev \
    libc6-dev \
    libdevmapper-dev \
    libglib2.0-dev \
    libgpgme-dev \
    libgpg-error-dev \
    libprotobuf-dev \
    libseccomp-dev \
    libselinux1-dev \
    libsystemd-dev \
    pkg-config \
    runc \
    uidmap \
    make

RUN git clone https://github.com/go-swagger/go-swagger.git && \
    cd go-swagger && go install ./cmd/swagger && cd -

RUN git clone https://github.com/containers/podman.git && \
    cd podman && make swagger && cd -
```
I think the difference is I didn't know I need to put the source folder in GOPATH.
